items.title: Elevated uric acid induces erectile dysfunction in rats by interacting with MLCK and inhibiting its ubiquitin-mediated degradation
items.author: Ti Zhou
items.content: Elevated uric acid induces early erectile dysfunction by interacting with MLCK and by inhibiting its ubiquitin-mediated degradation. MLCK inhibitor ML-7 and uric acid-lowering therapy improve erectile function induced by hyperuricemia.

items.title: Resynchronization of microglial activity in the brain is associated with restoration of motor function in Parkinson’s disease
items.author: Hongjun Jin
items.content: Brain PET-MRI imaging shows higher [18F]GSK uptake across multiple regions in A53T compared to WT mice. Correlation analyses reveal that resynchronization of microglia in Parkinson’s disease is associated with motor function restoration.

items.title: The role of environmentally mediated drug resistance in facilitating the spatial distribution of residual disease
items.author: Noemi Picco
items.content: Mechanistic modelling of tumour-stroma crosstalk sheds light on how targeted drug dynamics and microenvironmental heterogeneity shape residual disease through distinct mechanisms in the tumour microenvironment.

items.title: Disruption of TEX38 impairs sperm morphogenesis and the migration of sperm into the oviduct
items.author: Ying Zheng
items.content: TEX38, essential for sperm function in mice, interacts with ARRDC5 to regulate spermiogenesis, sperm migration and egg binding, suggesting that this interaction could be a target for male infertility treatment and contraception

items.title: Exploiting collateral sensitivity in the evolution of resistance to tyrosine kinase inhibitors in soft tissue sarcomas
items.author: Paul H. Huang
items.content: Sarcoma treatments are limited due to resistance. Analysis of clinically relevant tyrosine kinase inhibitors demonstrates that sarcoma cells develop collateral sensitivities to alternative drugs which can be exploited for adaptive therapies.

items.title: Rare variants and founder effect in the Beauce region of Quebec
items.author: Simon L. Girard
items.content: Fine-scale population analysis combining deep genealogical data and whole-genome sequencing reveals a regional founder effect in Beauce (Quebec, Canada) leading to an increase in frequency for 36 rare pathogenic variants.

items.title: Macrophagic Ym1 orchestrates γδT cell-derived IL-17 production and keratinocyte functionality to mediate psoriasis-like skin inflammation
items.author: Wenhua Zhu
items.content: Macrophagic Ym1 not only regulates γδT cell-derived IL-17 production but also directly influences keratinocyte functionality, thereby contributing to the development of psoriasis-like skin inflammation.

items.title: Molecular mechanism of β-arrestin 2 interaction with phosphorylated intracellular loop 3 of dopamine receptor D2
items.author: Ka Young Chung
items.content: Structural and biochemical studies reveal how β-arrestin 2 recognizes the phosphorylated ICL3 of D2 dopamine receptor, offering insights into GPCR-arrestin signaling in receptors lacking a C-terminal tail.
